title: Houghton-le-Spring (UK Parliament constituency)

infobox.value: [Houghton & Washington](/wiki/Houghton_and_Washington_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Houghton and Washington (UK Parliament constituency)") and [Easington](/wiki/Easington_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Easington (UK Parliament constituency)")[[1]](#cite_note-ElectionWeb74-1)

was a [county constituency](/wiki/County_constituency "County constituency") of the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") of the [Parliament of the United Kingdom](/wiki/Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom "Parliament of the United Kingdom") from 1885 to 1983. Centred on the town of [Houghton-le-Spring](/wiki/Houghton-le-Spring "Houghton-le-Spring"), now part of the [City of Sunderland](/wiki/City_of_Sunderland "City of Sunderland"), it elected one [Member of Parliament](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament_(United_Kingdom) "Member of Parliament (United Kingdom)") (MP) by the [first-past-the-post](/wiki/First-past-the-post "First-past-the-post") system of [election](/wiki/Election "Election").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Parliamentary constituency in the United Kingdom, 1885–1983

| Houghton-le-Spring | |
| --- | --- |
| Former [county constituency](/wiki/County_constituency "County constituency") for the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") | |
| [Outline map](/wiki/File:HoughtonLeSpring1974Constituency.svg) Boundary of Houghton-le-Spring in [County Durham](/wiki/County_Durham "County Durham"), boundaries 1974–1983 | |
| [County](/wiki/Ceremonial_counties_of_England "Ceremonial counties of England") | [County Durham](/wiki/County_Durham "County Durham") |
| Major settlements | [Houghton-le-Spring](/wiki/Houghton-le-Spring "Houghton-le-Spring") |
| [1885](/wiki/1885_United_Kingdom_general_election "1885 United Kingdom general election")–[1983](/wiki/1983_United_Kingdom_general_election "1983 United Kingdom general election") | |
| Seats | One |
| Created from | [South Durham](/wiki/South_Durham_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "South Durham (UK Parliament constituency)") |
| Replaced by | [Houghton & Washington](/wiki/Houghton_and_Washington_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Houghton and Washington (UK Parliament constituency)") and [Easington](/wiki/Easington_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Easington (UK Parliament constituency)")[[1]](#cite_note-ElectionWeb74-1) |

**Houghton-le-Spring** was a [county constituency](/wiki/County_constituency "County constituency") of the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") of the [Parliament of the United Kingdom](/wiki/Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom "Parliament of the United Kingdom") from 1885 to 1983. Centred on the town of [Houghton-le-Spring](/wiki/Houghton-le-Spring "Houghton-le-Spring"), now part of the [City of Sunderland](/wiki/City_of_Sunderland "City of Sunderland"), it elected one [Member of Parliament](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament_(United_Kingdom) "Member of Parliament (United Kingdom)") (MP) by the [first-past-the-post](/wiki/First-past-the-post "First-past-the-post") system of [election](/wiki/Election "Election").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houghton-le-Spring_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Creation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houghton-le-Spring_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Creation")]

The constituency was created for the [1885 general election](/wiki/1885_United_Kingdom_general_election "1885 United Kingdom general election") by the [Redistribution of Seats Act 1885](/wiki/Redistribution_of_Seats_Act_1885 "Redistribution of Seats Act 1885") as one of eight new single-member divisions of the county of Durham, replacing the two 2-member seats of [North Durham](/wiki/North_Durham_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "North Durham (UK Parliament constituency)") and [South Durham](/wiki/South_Durham_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "South Durham (UK Parliament constituency)").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) The seat included the towns of [Houghton-le-Spring](/wiki/Houghton-le-Spring "Houghton-le-Spring"), [Hetton-le-Hole](/wiki/Hetton-le-Hole "Hetton-le-Hole") and [Seaham](/wiki/Seaham "Seaham") and areas to the south and west of the borough of Sunderland. The majority now lies within the [City of Sunderland](/wiki/City_of_Sunderland "City of Sunderland") in the metropolitan county of [Tyne and Wear](/wiki/Tyne_and_Wear "Tyne and Wear").

### Boundaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houghton-le-Spring_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Boundaries")]

#### 1885–1918

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houghton-le-Spring_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 1885–1918")]

* The Sessional Divisions of Houghton-le-Spring, Sunderland and Seaham Harbour (part); and
* The Municipal Borough of Sunderland[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

NB included only non-resident freeholders in the parliamentary borough of Sunderland.

*See map on Vision of Britain website.*[[3]](#cite_note-3)

#### 1918–1950

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houghton-le-Spring_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 1918–1950")]

* The Urban District of Houghton-le-Spring;
* the Rural Districts of South Shields and Sunderland; and
* part of the Rural District of Houghton-le-Spring.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

*Rural/coastal areas between South Shields and Sunderland, including [the Boldons](/wiki/The_Boldons "The Boldons"), transferred from [Jarrow](/wiki/Jarrow_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Jarrow (UK Parliament constituency)").  Lost Hetton-le-Hole to the new [Durham](/wiki/City_of_Durham_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "City of Durham (UK Parliament constituency)") Division of County Durham and Seaham to the new [Seaham](/wiki/Seaham_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Seaham (UK Parliament constituency)") Division.*

#### 1950–1955

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houghton-le-Spring_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 1950–1955")]

* The Urban Districts of Houghton-le-Spring and Seaham; and
* the Rural District of Sunderland.[[5]](#cite_note-:1-5)

*Seaham (including Seaham Harbour) transferred back from the abolished Seaham Division. Lost areas to the Borough Constituencies of [South Shields](/wiki/South_Shields_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "South Shields (UK Parliament constituency)"), [Sunderland North](/wiki/Sunderland_North_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Sunderland North (UK Parliament constituency)") and [Sunderland South](/wiki/Sunderland_South_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Sunderland South (UK Parliament constituency)") as a result of the expansion of the respective County Boroughs. Remaining northern areas, largely comprising the Urban District of Boldon (which had largely succeeded the abolished Rural District of South Shields), transferred to Jarrow.*

#### 1955–1974

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houghton-le-Spring_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 1955–1974")]

* The Urban Districts of Houghton-le-Spring and Seaham; and
* the Rural District of Sunderland except the parts of the parishes of Ford, Herrington, Hylton, and Silksworth added to the County Borough of Sunderland by the [Sunderland Extension Act 1950](/w/index.php?title=Sunderland_Extension_Act_1950&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sunderland Extension Act 1950 (page does not exist)") ([14 Geo. 6](/wiki/14_Geo._6 "14 Geo. 6"). c. liv).[[5]](#cite_note-:1-5)

#### 1974–1983

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houghton-le-Spring_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 1974–1983")]

* The Urban Districts of Hetton, Houghton-le-Spring, and Seaham; and
* in the Rural District of Easington, the parishes of Burdon, Cold Hesledon, Dalton-le-Dale, East Murton, Seaton with Slingley, and Warden Law.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

*The Urban District of Hetton transferred back from Durham and northern parts of the Rural District of Easington, including East [Murton](/wiki/Murton,_County_Durham "Murton, County Durham"), transferred from [Easington](/wiki/Easington_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Easington (UK Parliament constituency)"). Parts comprising the former Rural District of Sunderland, which had been largely absorbed by the County Borough, were transferred to Sunderland North (Hylton) and Sunderland South ([Ryhope](/wiki/Ryhope "Ryhope") and [Silksworth](/wiki/New_Silksworth "New Silksworth")).*

### Abolition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Houghton-le-Spring_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Abolition")]

The seat was abolished for the [1983 general election](/wiki/1983_United_Kingdom_general_election "1983 United Kingdom general election") as a result of the periodic review of parliamentary constituencies following the re-organisation of local government under the [Local Government Act 1972](/wiki/Local_Government_Act_1972 "Local Government Act 1972"). On abolition, Seaham and East Murton, which had been retained within the county of Durham and comprised about 40% of the electorate, were returned to the Easington constituency. The remainder, including Houghton-le-Spring and Hetton-le-Hole, was included in the new constituency of [Houghton and Washington](/wiki/Houghton_and_Washington_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Houghton and Washington (UK Parliament constituency)") in the county of Tyne and Wear.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

**General Election 1914–15**:

Another General Election was required to take place before the end of 1915. The political parties had been making preparations for an election to take place and by July 1914, the following candidates had been selected;

* **Liberal**: [Thomas Wing](/wiki/Thomas_Wing_(Liberal_politician) "Thomas Wing (Liberal politician)")
* **Unionist**:
* **Labour**: [W. P. Richardson](/wiki/W._P._Richardson_(trade_unionist) "W. P. Richardson (trade unionist)")
